title: Force sync ?
author: Fred278
body:
Hello ! 

I am using your lib, no issue. I was able to publish my app.
However, each version I tested before publishing, I faced a sync issue. I only have 1 keyvalue to sync so my use case is quite simple.
My iOS device sets the value and tvOS version is reading it.

When I display the _CloudStorageSync.status_ I got _"Local change"_ 
So I assume my iOS device is not sync'ing the data. 
Is there a way to force ? or clean something ? or just debug?

I tried to configure the project again, Cloudstorage, I checked my iCloud account, restarted all devices.
Just suddenly, everything is working again without changing the code, and I don't know why.

Thank you !

title: EXC_BREAKPOINT crash in com.apple.kvs.client.callback queue
author: Tabonx

title: "Return from initializer without initializing all stored properties" with Swift 6 language mode
author: flexlixrup
body: When having an explicit initializer in a class, the CloudStorage property wrapper requires to be initialized explicitly in Swift 6 language mode. This is likely attributed to the feature flag "Isolated Global Variables" and the global `private let sync = CloudStorageSync.shared` in the CloudStorage code.

title: @CloudStorage doesn't workin inside @Observable
author: iband
body: Basically the same issue as with not being able to use @AppStorage inside @Observable (workaround for @AppStorage was suggested here https://medium.com/@davidsteppenbeck/accessing-userdefaults-within-observable-classes-in-ios-17-8cb46ca75689)

title: Store optional enums (Int and String)
author: iband

title: Missing support for arrays
author: mac-cain13
body: The [key-value store also supports arrays](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/foundation/nsubiquitouskeyvaluestore/1417721-set), very convenient to store for example the ordering of certain things. But this doesn't seem to be supported by CloudStorage at the moment, would be great to add support for this.

title: SwiftUI Threading Issue
author: DandyLyons
body: When I run CloudStorage I receive the following error. `Publishing changes from background threads is not allowed; make sure to publish values from the main thread (via operators like receive(on:)) on model updates.` Perhaps `status = ` should also be inside of the DispatchQueue ?

title: Doesn't work with `suiteName` UserDefaults
author: simanerush
body:
I want to be able to use `@CloudStorage` with an extra parameter `store` where I can specify the `UserDefaults` store. For example, I can write
```swift
@AppStorage("myKey", store: UserDefaults(suiteName: "com.myName")) var userPreference = Color.red
```

I really want `@CloudStorage` to have this functionality, too!

title: '…' is only available in tvOS 13.0 or newer
author: grgar
body:
Reproduction steps:

1. Create a new Xcode 12.4 tvOS SwiftUI app template.
2. Set deployment target to tvOS 13.0.  
![Screenshot 2021-03-27 at 10 00 20 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/191085/112717302-9f85d280-8ee3-11eb-9f3d-5edb788e1261.png)
3. Add CloudStorage Swift package.
4. `import CloudStorage` somewhere.
5. Build
6. 💥  
![Screenshot 2021-03-27 at 10 05 55 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/191085/112717365-11f6b280-8ee4-11eb-91ba-57899b7ee6dd.png)

I've tried:

- Adding `.tvOS(.v13)` platform to CloudStorage Package.swift.  
    ```diff
    diff --git a/Package.swift b/Package.swift
    index 29e780d..769993d 100644
    --- a/Package.swift
    +++ b/Package.swift
    @@ -6,6 +6,7 @@ let package = Package(
         platforms: [
             .macOS(.v10_15),
             .iOS(.v13),
    +        .tvOS(.v13),
         ],
         products: [
             .library(name: "CloudStorage", targets: ["CloudStorage"]),
    ```

Any ideas what's up?
